span.p: GUI shape — one row per display

color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
position 1 → A: Code
position 2 → A: Code
position 3 → — None
position 4 → Group B
Letter (A) is the stable internal ID.
Label ('Code') is optional, user-set,
display-only. Pool of 10 letters A..J
always exists; no creation/deletion.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Conversion is one-way at runtime.
JSON is canonical, engine is derived.
list-of-lists never mutated by hand.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Single-group constraint is structural.
groupOf is a map keyed by position.
A position has ONE group letter.
Cannot represent overlap by construction.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Why a fixed pool of 10 letters?
Mac Studio M2 Ultra tops out at 8 displays;
realistic max useful sync groups is
floor(8/2) = 4. A pool of 10 covers every
realistic configuration with room.
The fixed cap eliminates a class of code:
- no 'next unused letter' assignment
- no retired-letter tracking
- no creation or dissolution events
- no '+ New group' UI affordance
Empty groups remain in the pool, inert.
Their letter and label persist across
emptiness — the user can pre-label
before assigning members.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Why is spaceNames keyed by letter,
not by display or position?
Earlier draft kept names in hs.settings
keyed by sorted-position-tuple ('1,2,3').
Reshuffling groupOf silently stranded
names under stale keys.
Letter-keyed names follow the group
identity automatically. Reshuffles
change positions IN a group, not the
group letter, so names stay attached.
groupLabels lets users set an optional
display name alongside the letter,
also keyed by letter for the same reason.

svg.text: SpacesSync data model (revised — stable letter IDs)
